title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: datadiff R package comparing visualizing differences data frames. provides row--row comparison, column metadata diffing, context-aware output, HTML report generation via flexdashboard.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Run all tests make test  # Run tests for specific source files (maps to corresponding test files) make test R/compare.R make test R/equal.R R/diffdata.R  # Lint the package make lint  # Run R CMD check make check  # Generate documentation (roxygen2) make document  # Snapshot renv (captures all deps including Suggests) make snapshot  # Restore renv packages from lockfile make restore  # Format package code make format

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/compare.R - Main comparison logic: compare_data() - Entry point data frame comparison, returns diff context rows compare_join() - Full outer join row number, identifies rows x , y , compare_diff() - Identifies differences, applies context rows, pivots output compare_groups() - Compares group membership data frames compare_columns() - Detects column name/type differences data frames R/equal.R - is_equal() vectorized equality test handling NA, Inf, numeric tolerance R/diff.R - HTML rendering: show_diff() - Formats diff HTML table color coding (red=deletions, green=additions) render_diff() - Renders diff flexdashboard, opens RStudio viewer browser R/diffdata.R - diffdata() high-level API combining comparison rendering R/utils.R - Helper utilities (col_class() type detection)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: diffdata() validates inputs checks column compatibility via compare_columns() compare_data() joins frames row number identifies differences compare_diff() adds context rows pivots x/y values display render_diff() generates styled HTML via formattable/kableExtra opens viewer

title: CLAUDE.md
text: dplyr, tidyr, purrr, stringr - Data manipulation formattable, kableExtra - Table styling flexdashboard, rmarkdown - HTML report generation cli, glue - User messaging

title: CLAUDE.md
text: encountering friction commands (special flags needed, multi-step processes, non-obvious incantations): - Propose adding Makefile targets repeatable operations - Propose updating CLAUDE.md context help future sessions keeps project self-documenting avoids hitting issues repeatedly.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Follow tidyverse style guide: snake_case functions/variables Use |> pipe operator data transformations Use roxygen2 @param, @return, @export tags Input validation stopifnot() user-facing functions Use rlang::abort() cli::cli_alert_* error messaging

title: Pre-CRAN Roadmap
text: 2026-07-03 project roundup (seven parallel assessments: validation, dataCompareR compat, pkgdown, performance, style, code review, competitive positioning). Status 2026-07-04: Phases 0-4 complete. Phase 5 nearly done — version 0.1.0, NEWS.md, cran-comments.md written; R CMD check ---cran clean (0/0/0); CI green 5 platforms; pkgdown site live https://thays42.github.io/datadiffr/. Remaining submission: win-builder + rhub, submit. 547 tests passing locally.

title: Pre-CRAN Roadmap

Still user-side: rename GitHub repo (gh repo rename datadiffr) optionally local directory, update DESCRIPTION URL/BugReports new slug. Key-based matching — decided scope shipped (= compare_data()/diffdata()).

title: Pre-CRAN Roadmap
text: Delete foo()/bar()/browser() debug leftovers R/diff.R. Delete test_debug.R coverage.xml; gitignore coverage output. Fix renv: later 1.4.2 compile Fedora 44 glibc; lockfile now pins later 1.4.8. make test/make lint work . Commit pending .lintr / .gitignore changes.

title: Pre-CRAN Roadmap
text: reproduced bugs fixed test-first (see git log details): - [x] B1 empty diff crash — diffdata(x, x) now reports “differences found”. - [x] B2 -NA added/deleted rows silently dropped — join-type rows always reported. - [x] B3 grouped tibbles produced garbage — inputs ungrouped join. - [x] B4 factors different level sets errored — compared character. - [x] B5 user column named .rn clobbered — internal join key namespaced. - [x] B6 list-columns errored — compared element-wise via identical(). - [x] B7 raw tidyr errors type conflicts / shared columns — clear cli errors. - [x] B8 context_cols rejected bare names — selections resolved via tidyselect::eval_select() entry points (bare forwarding never chains). - [x] B9 truncated differences reappeared disguised context rows. - [x] B10 NA vs NaN now compare unequal (documented; matches rCompare semantics). - [x] B12 compare_groups() guards in_x/in_y grouping columns. - [x] B13 is_equal() honors vector contract; errors incompatible lengths. - [x] B14 tolerance applies Date/POSIXct (days/seconds scale). - [x] B15 compare_columns() stable column order + schema empty results. - [x] B16 render_diff(output_file=) errors target directory missing. - [x] B17 truncation message uses cli pluralization; “differing rows” semantics documented. - [x] Mis-targeted diffdata edge-case test re-pointed real diff path. - Note: suspected --one old compare.R:106 unreachable (truncation guarantees later diff exists), indexing made robust anyway rowSums refactor. Remaining test gaps (fine grow organically): - [x] show_diff() now direct output assertions (test-show-diff.R: block separators, cell colouring, column borders; test-diff-class.R: B11 tolerance).

title: Pre-CRAN Roadmap
text: Key-based matching: = argument compare_data() diffdata(); keys must exist frames unique per frame; output ordered key; keys always included output. Positional matching remains default. checkmate validation across exported functions; closed gaps (output_file, max_differences >= 0, context_rows integerish/non-negative, render_diff() asserts required diff columns). Aligned compare_data()/diffdata() argument order (formals-equality test pins ). Differing max_differences defaults kept documented (Inf vs 10). Renamed is_equal(tol =) → tolerance. Perf: apply() → rowSums()/colSums() mask reductions. Dropped glue, fs, lubridate dependencies; added checkmate, rlang, tidyselect (previously transitive). Classed diff object (datadiff_diff subclass tbl_df) carrying tolerance, n_differences, truncated, diff_columns attributes, print() summary() methods (R/diff-class.R). compare_data() returns ; headless users get console output. Fixes B11: show_diff() now reads tolerance object instead re-deriving cell mask default tolerance. Renderer perf: removed two O(n^2) kableExtra offenders — pack_rows() (one call per block, replaced single row_spec() border rule blocks) column_spec() (per-<td> rewrite, replaced scoped <style> rule self-contained lightable table). ~1.5k diff rows dropped ~53s ~7s. Remaining cost row_spec() background calls (still O(n^2), smaller constant); full vectorized HTML rebuild go deferred — changes whole visual path wants interactive visual verification. bites users raise max_differences. Decomposed compare_diff() diff_mask() / limit_differences() / context_indices() helpers (R/compare.R). Pure refactor; behavior unchanged. Optional perf (deferred, low value): replace positional hash join frame padding (measured 55x join step alone, whole compare path already ~1s 500k rows, join bottleneck; renderer ). worth rewrite risk user-visible gain.

title: Pre-CRAN Roadmap
text: Clean-room reimplementation (dataCompareR Apache-2.0, package MIT — code reuse; behavior pinned black-box probes dataCompareR 0.1.4). dataCompareR stays Suggests parity oracle tests. rCompare() native comparison path returning c("datadiff_compare", "dataCompareRobject") exact field shapes; faithful defaults (exact equality, error mismatches cap) plus tolerance = extension appended original arguments. Cleaned frames ride along attribute render_diff() bridge. print(), summary() (33 fields, dynamic column names), print.summary (markdown report incl. Dropped Rows Details), saveReport() (rmarkdown, writes .Rmd/.md/.html), generateMismatchData() (keyed keyless). Documented improvement: detail tables deterministic (sorted |diff| desc) dataCompareR sampled. Parity test suite (test-rcompare-parity.R): field--field vs real dataCompareR shared fixtures, skip_if_not_installed. Bridge: render_diff() now S3 generic datadiff_compare method. Vignette: “Migrating dataCompareR” (vignettes/). Note: local make check needs qpdf installed (sudo dnf install qpdf) now package builds vignettes; without check reports 1 environmental WARNING. CRAN builders qpdf.

title: Pre-CRAN Roadmap

README.Rmd added .Rbuildignore. @examples exported functions — runnable examples compare_data()/compare_groups()/compare_columns()/is_equal(); @examplesIf interactive() diffdata()/render_diff() (open HTML report). rcompare surface already examples. checking examples ... OK. “Get started” vignette — vignettes/datadiffr.Rmd (pkgdown surfaces package-named vignette “Get started” link): core workflow, context rows, key matching, tolerance, column comparison, HTML report. chunks verified real output. URL: + BugReports: DESCRIPTION — repo renamed thays42/datadiffr; DESCRIPTION URL/BugReports README install line point new slug, plus pkgdown site URL. CI: added .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml (check-standard matrix) R-CMD-check badge. Test-coverage workflow skipped now (needs Codecov token). pkgdown: _pkgdown.yml curated reference index (High-level API / Comparison / Rendering / dataCompareR compatibility); check_pkgdown() reports problems. Added .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml deploy workflow. Removed stale empty docs/. Repo-side step still needed: enable GitHub Pages (gh-pages branch) pkgdown workflow can publish. Style pass: cli_abort render_diff, @noRd internals, stale globalVariables, markdown Rd bullets, test naming. (lint + air format clean.) Split remaining test hygiene: diffdata validation mega-test split; consider skip_if_not_installed patterns Suggests usage grows. (Optional, non-blocking.)

title: Pre-CRAN Roadmap

NEWS.md written (initial-release feature summary) version bumped 0.0.0.9000 → 0.1.0. Plain R CMD check still clean (0 errors / 0 notes / qpdf WARNING ). R CMD check ---cran clean locally — 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes (qpdf installed). “New submission” NOTE appears CRAN’s incoming-feasibility step (needs network), archived-dataCompareR Suggests NOTE documented cran-comments. Spell check set (inst/WORDLIST, tests/spelling.R, Language: en-US); spell_check_package() clean; British spellings switched American. cran-comments.md — documents “New submission” + archived-dataCompareR Suggests (conditional oracle via skip_if_not_installed). CI green: R-CMD-check passes 5 platforms (ubuntu devel/release/oldrel-1, macOS, Windows); pkgdown builds site live https://thays42.github.io/datadiffr/. dataCompareR (archived) installed CI CRAN archive; parity tests skip can’t run modern dplyr (skip_unless_oracle helper). Submit CRAN (devtools::submit_cran() web form).

title: Pre-CRAN Roadmap
text: Niche: “actively maintained package context-aware, report-quality HTML data diffs.” Unique among maintained packages: configurable context rows; polished HTML report default output. dataCompareR archived 2026-02, compareDF maintenance mode — report-oriented lane open. Honest weaknesses fix disclose: positional-matching (fixed: =), refuses diff column difference (consider diffing intersection), console print method (classed object work), aggressive max_differences = 10 default (documented).

title: Get started with datadiffr
text: Start two versions small table. compare_data() returns differences tidy data frame. result carries bookkeeping columns alongside data: .row — row number difference came . .join_type — whether row "x", "y", "". .diff_type — whether row "diff" "context" row. .source — diff rows, whether "x" () "y" () version; NA context rows. changed row appears twice — value value — can read change top bottom, like unified diff. object prints one-line summary table (, two changed rows), handy working plain console rather HTML report.
code: before <- data.frame(   id    = 1:6,   name  = c("Ana", "Ben", "Cai", "Dana", "Eve", "Finn"),   score = c(88, 91, 75, 82, 95, 60) ) after <- data.frame(   id    = 1:6,   name  = c("Ana", "Ben", "Cai", "Dana", "Eve", "Finn"),   score = c(88, 91, 79, 82, 95, 72) )  compare_data(before, after) #> datadiff: 2 changed, 0 added, 0 removed rows across 1 column #> Tolerance: 1.49011611938477e-08 #>  #> # A tibble: 8 × 7 #>    .row .join_type .diff_type .source    id name  score #>   <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>   <int> <chr> <dbl> #> 1     1 both       context    NA          1 Ana      88 #> 2     2 both       context    NA          2 Ben      91 #> 3     3 both       diff       x           3 Cai      75 #> 4     3 both       diff       y           3 Cai      79 #> 5     4 both       context    NA          4 Dana     82 #> 6     5 both       context    NA          5 Eve      95 #> 7     6 both       diff       x           6 Finn     60 #> 8     6 both       diff       y           6 Finn     72

title: Get started with datadiffr
text: default datadiffr surrounds change three unchanged rows . context_rows takes length-two vector — rows , rows — can widen, narrow, drop context entirely. can also restrict columns shown context context_cols, using tidyselect syntax (example context_cols = c(id, name)).
code: # No context: only the rows that actually changed compare_data(before, after, context_rows = c(0L, 0L)) #> datadiff: 2 changed, 0 added, 0 removed rows across 1 column #> Tolerance: 1.49011611938477e-08 #>  #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>    .row .join_type .diff_type .source    id name  score #>   <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>   <int> <chr> <dbl> #> 1     3 both       diff       x           3 Cai      75 #> 2     3 both       diff       y           3 Cai      79 #> 3     6 both       diff       x           6 Finn     60 #> 4     6 both       diff       y           6 Finn     72 # One row of context on each side compare_data(before, after, context_rows = c(1L, 1L)) #> datadiff: 2 changed, 0 added, 0 removed rows across 1 column #> Tolerance: 1.49011611938477e-08 #>  #> # A tibble: 7 × 7 #>    .row .join_type .diff_type .source    id name  score #>   <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>   <int> <chr> <dbl> #> 1     2 both       context    NA          2 Ben      91 #> 2     3 both       diff       x           3 Cai      75 #> 3     3 both       diff       y           3 Cai      79 #> 4     4 both       context    NA          4 Dana     82 #> 5     5 both       context    NA          5 Eve      95 #> 6     6 both       diff       x           6 Finn     60 #> 7     6 both       diff       y           6 Finn     72

title: Get started with datadiffr
text: examples match rows position. rows can move — sort changed, rows inserted deleted — match one key columns instead =. Keys must exist frames identify rows uniquely; output ordered key always includes . Rows present one frame always reported: id 3 shows removed (x ) id 7 added (y ).
code: # `after` is shuffled and has a new row (id 7) and a dropped row (id 3) after_shuffled <- data.frame(   id    = c(2L, 1L, 4L, 5L, 6L, 7L),   name  = c("Ben", "Ana", "Dana", "Eve", "Finn", "Gwen"),   score = c(91, 88, 82, 95, 72, 70) )  compare_data(before, after_shuffled, by = "id", context_rows = c(0L, 0L)) #> datadiff: 1 changed, 1 added, 1 removed row across 2 columns #> Tolerance: 1.49011611938477e-08 #>  #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>    .row .join_type .diff_type .source    id name  score #>   <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>   <int> <chr> <dbl> #> 1     3 x          diff       x           3 Cai      75 #> 2     6 both       diff       x           6 Finn     60 #> 3     6 both       diff       y           6 Finn     72 #> 4     7 y          diff       y           7 Gwen     70

title: Get started with datadiffr
text: Floating-point data rarely bit-identical. tolerance treats numeric values within threshold equal, tiny differences don’t flood report. tolerance argument available diffdata() is_equal(), applies natural scale dates (days) datetimes (seconds).
code: measured  <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1.0000, 2.0000, 3.0000)) recomputed <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1.0001, 2.5000, 3.0000))  # Exact-ish comparison flags both id 1 and id 2 compare_data(measured, recomputed, context_rows = c(0L, 0L)) #> datadiff: 2 changed, 0 added, 0 removed rows across 1 column #> Tolerance: 1.49011611938477e-08 #>  #> # A tibble: 4 × 6 #>    .row .join_type .diff_type .source    id value #>   <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>   <int> <dbl> #> 1     1 both       diff       x           1  1    #> 2     1 both       diff       y           1  1.00 #> 3     2 both       diff       x           2  2    #> 4     2 both       diff       y           2  2.5  # A tolerance of 0.01 keeps only the genuine change (id 2) compare_data(measured, recomputed, tolerance = 0.01, context_rows = c(0L, 0L)) #> datadiff: 1 changed, 0 added, 0 removed rows across 1 column #> Tolerance: 0.01 #>  #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>    .row .join_type .diff_type .source    id value #>   <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>   <int> <dbl> #> 1     2 both       diff       x           2   2   #> 2     2 both       diff       y           2   2.5

title: Get started with datadiffr
text: datadiffr compares values, expects two frames share columns compatible types. don’t, reports column differences instead trying diff values. can inspect directly compare_columns(). value changed type (integer double) note exists x.
code: x <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = 1:3, note = c("a", "b", "c")) y <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5))  compare_columns(x, y) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   .diff         column x_type    y_type  #>   <chr>         <chr>  <chr>     <chr>   #> 1 in x only     note   character NA      #> 2 type conflict value  integer   numeric

title: Get started with datadiffr
text: diffdata() runs comparison opens result styled, self-contained HTML report — changed rows colored red () green (), context rows grayed . interactive session opens RStudio viewer browser: save report instead opening , pass output_file: diffdata() defaults max_differences = 10 keep reports fast render; raise need every difference report. compare_data(), meant programmatic use, reports everything default. already diff compare_data(), render directly render_diff():
code: diffdata(before, after) diffdata(before, after, output_file = "diff-report.html") diff <- compare_data(before, after) render_diff(diff)

title: Get started with datadiffr
text: datadiffr also ships clean-room drop-replacement archived dataCompareR package — rCompare() friends. See vignette("migrating--datacomparer").

title: Migrating from dataCompareR
text: full report, : Everything downstream works way:
code: library(datadiffr)  orders_old <- data.frame(   order_id = 1:5,   amount = c(10, 20.5, 30, 45, 50),   status = c("open", "open", "closed", "open", "closed") ) orders_new <- data.frame(   order_id = c(1:4, 6L),   amount = c(10, 21.0, 30, 45, 60),   status = c("open", "paid", "closed", "open", "open") )  cmp <- rCompare(orders_old, orders_new, keys = "order_id") cmp #> All columns were compared, 2 row(s) were dropped from comparison #> There are 2 mismatched variables: #> First and last 5 observations for the 2 mismatched variables #>   ORDER_ID valueA valueB variable     typeA     typeB diffAB #> 1        2   20.5     21   AMOUNT    double    double   -0.5 #> 2        2   open   paid   STATUS character character summary(cmp) #>  #> Data Comparison #> =============== #>  #> Date comparison run: 2026-07-04 20:41:49   #> Comparison run on R version 4.6.1 (2026-06-24)   #> With datadiffr version 0.1.0   #>  #> Meta Summary #> ============ #>  #> |Dataset Name |Number of Rows |Number of Columns | #> |:------------|:--------------|:-----------------| #> |orders_old   |5              |3                 | #> |orders_new   |5              |3                 | #>  #> Variable Summary #> ================ #>  #> Number of columns in common: 3   #> Number of columns only in orders_old: 0   #> Number of columns only in orders_new: 0   #> Number of columns with a type mismatch: 0   #> Match keys : 1   - ORDER_ID #>  #>  #> Row Summary #> =========== #>  #> Total number of rows read from orders_old: 5   #> Total number of rows read from orders_new: 5   #> Number of rows in common: 4   #> Number of rows dropped from orders_old: 1   #> Number of rows dropped from orders_new: 1   #>  #>  #> Data Values Comparison Summary #> ============================== #>  #> Number of columns compared with ALL rows equal: 0   #> Number of columns compared with SOME rows unequal: 2   #> Number of columns with missing value differences: 0   #>  #> Summary of columns with some rows unequal:  #>  #> |Column |Type (in orders_old) |Type (in orders_new) | # differences|Max difference | # NAs| #> |:------|:--------------------|:--------------------|-------------:|:--------------|-----:| #> |AMOUNT |double               |double               |             1|0.5            |     0| #> |STATUS |character            |character            |             1|NA             |     0| #>  #>  #> Unequal column details #> ====================== #>  #> #### Column -  AMOUNT #>  #> | ORDER_ID| AMOUNT (orders_old)| AMOUNT (orders_new)|Type (orders_old) |Type (orders_new) | Difference| #> |--------:|-------------------:|-------------------:|:-----------------|:-----------------|----------:| #> |        2|                20.5|                  21|double            |double            |       -0.5| #>  #> #### Column -  STATUS #>  #> | ORDER_ID|STATUS (orders_old) |STATUS (orders_new) |Type (orders_old) |Type (orders_new) |Difference | #> |--------:|:-------------------|:-------------------|:-----------------|:-----------------|:----------| #> |        2|open                |paid                |character         |character         |           | #>  #> Dropped Rows Details #> ==================== #>  #> The following rows were dropped from orders_old #>  #> | ORDER_ID| #> |--------:| #> |        5| #>  #> The following rows were dropped from orders_new #>  #> | ORDER_ID| #> |--------:| #> |        6| mismatches <- generateMismatchData(cmp, orders_old, orders_new) mismatches$orders_old_mm #>   ORDER_ID AMOUNT STATUS #> 2        2   20.5   open saveReport(cmp, reportName = "orders", reportLocation = ".")

title: Migrating from dataCompareR
text: returned object class c("datadiff_compare", "dataCompareRobject") six fields (meta, colMatching, rowMatching, cleaninginfo, mismatches, matches) shapes, code inspects object — cmp$mismatches$AMOUNT, cmp$rowMatching$inboth, — keeps working. comparison semantics preserved, including corners: Column names cleaned make.names() matched case-insensitively; shared columns reported upper-case sorted. Without keys, rows compared position longer frame truncated (recorded rowMatching$inA$indices_removed). keys, key columns must exist frames (checked case-sensitively cleaned names) must identify rows uniquely frame. NA matches NA NaN matches NaN, NA match NaN. Factors compared character, coercion recorded cleaninginfo. Columns whose classes differ frames (example integer vs double) reported mismatching every row, types shown blank difference. mismatches cap errors exceeded — truncate — exactly dataCompareR behaved.

title: Migrating from dataCompareR
text: deliberate documented; none change object contract: Detail tables deterministic. dataCompareR sampled rows summary() detail tables beyond mismatchCount, datadiffr sorts decreasing absolute difference keeps first rows. Reruns produce identical reports. Metadata references datadiffr. summary()$version datadiffr version, report headers say “datadiffr version …”. Clear validation errors. Inputs must data frames (dataCompareR silently coerced malformed inputs empty comparisons), error messages worded differently, though fire situations.

title: Migrating from dataCompareR
text: rCompare() gains tolerance argument, appended original arguments positional calls behave identically. dataCompareR’s fuzziness mechanism roundDigits (round sides, compare exactly), fails values round different directions: default tolerance = 0 keeps faithful exact comparison. second extension render_diff(): datadiffr’s styled HTML diff report, straight comparison object.
code: a <- data.frame(x = 1.14) b <- data.frame(x = 1.16) # rounds to 1.1 vs 1.2: reported as a mismatch length(rCompare(a, b, roundDigits = 1)$mismatches) #> [1] 1 # absolute tolerance: equal length(rCompare(a, b, tolerance = 0.05)$mismatches) #> [1] 0 render_diff(cmp)                          # opens in the viewer render_diff(cmp, output_file = "cmp.html") # or saves to a file

title: Migrating from dataCompareR
text: compatibility layer stable endpoint — can stay . datadiffr’s native API adds things rCompare() express: diffdata() compares, opens HTML diff report added rows green, removed rows red, unchanged context rows orientation. compare_data() returns underlying diff data frame programmatic use.
code: diffdata(   orders_old,   orders_new,   by = "order_id",        # key-based matching, like rCompare keys   context_rows = c(2, 2), # unified-diff style context around changes   context_cols = c(order_id, amount),   tolerance = 0.01 )

title: Migrating from dataCompareR
text: “Key … found data frames” — key names checked make.names()-cleaned column names, case-sensitively: keys = "ID" find column named id. matches dataCompareR. “Detected N mismatches, exceeds mismatches cap” — cap guard, limit; raise drop argument, dataCompareR. generateMismatchData() says frame part original comparison — like dataCompareR, frames matched names passed rCompare() time; pass variables. render_diff() asks flexdashboard — HTML diff report needs suggested flexdashboard package: install.packages("flexdashboard").

title: Authors and Citation
text: Tyler Hays. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hays T (2026). datadiffr: Compare Data Frames Render HTML Diff Reports. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/thays42/datadiffr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {datadiffr: Compare Data Frames and Render HTML Diff Reports},   author = {Tyler Hays},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/thays42/datadiffr}, }

what: Compare Data Frames and Render HTML Diff Reports
title: Compare Data Frames and Render HTML Diff Reports
text: datadiffr compares two data frames shows differences way code review shows diff: cell cell, context, styled HTML report. Changed rows lined old--new, colored red green, unchanged rows around change context. fills report-oriented lane data comparison. waldo diffdf excellent console text output; datadiffr targets case want self-contained HTML report read, save, share — stays actively maintained now dataCompareR archived compareDF maintenance mode.

title: Compare Data Frames and Render HTML Diff Reports
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("thays42/datadiffr")

title: Compare Data Frames and Render HTML Diff Reports
text: compare_data() returns differences tidy data frame, context rows around change. Rows matched position default, key columns =. diffdata() runs comparison opens HTML report RStudio viewer browser (writes file output_file =):
code: library(datadiffr)  before <- data.frame(   id    = 1:6,   name  = c("Ana", "Ben", "Cai", "Dana", "Eve", "Finn"),   score = c(88, 91, 75, 82, 95, 60),   grade = c("B", "A", "C", "B", "A", "D") ) after <- data.frame(   id    = 1:6,   name  = c("Ana", "Ben", "Cai", "Dana", "Eve", "Finn"),   score = c(88, 91, 79, 82, 95, 72),   grade = c("B", "A", "C", "B", "A", "C") )  compare_data(before, after, context_rows = c(1L, 1L)) #> datadiff: 2 changed, 0 added, 0 removed rows across 2 columns #> Tolerance: 1.49011611938477e-08 #>  #> # A tibble: 7 × 8 #>    .row .join_type .diff_type .source    id name  score grade #>   <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>   <int> <chr> <dbl> <chr> #> 1     2 both       context    <NA>        2 Ben      91 A     #> 2     3 both       diff       x           3 Cai      75 C     #> 3     3 both       diff       y           3 Cai      79 C     #> 4     4 both       context    <NA>        4 Dana     82 B     #> 5     5 both       context    <NA>        5 Eve      95 A     #> 6     6 both       diff       x           6 Finn     60 D     #> 7     6 both       diff       y           6 Finn     72 C diffdata(before, after)

title: Compare Data Frames and Render HTML Diff Reports
text: datadiffr maintained option built around report-quality HTML diff configurable context rows. refuses diff frames whose columns differ name type, reporting column differences instead (see compare_columns()).

title: Compare Data Frames and Render HTML Diff Reports
text: datadiffr ships clean-room rCompare() friends (generateMismatchData(), saveReport()) object shape archived dataCompareR, existing scripts keep working. See vignette("migrating--datacomparer").

title: Compare Data Frames and Render HTML Diff Reports
text: MIT © Tyler Hays

what: Compare column metadata between two data frames — compare_columns
title: Compare column metadata between two data frames — compare_columns
text: Compare column metadata two data frames

title: Compare column metadata between two data frames — compare_columns
code: compare_columns(x, y)

title: Compare column metadata between two data frames — compare_columns
text: x, y Data frames compare.

title: Compare column metadata between two data frames — compare_columns
text: data frame following columns: .diff - type difference: "x ", "y ", "type conflict" column - column name x_type - column type x (applicable) y_type - column type y (applicable) Returns empty data frame differences.

title: Compare column metadata between two data frames — compare_columns
code: x <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = 1:3, extra = letters[1:3]) y <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5))  # `value` differs in type (integer vs double) and `extra` is only in x compare_columns(x, y) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   .diff         column x_type    y_type  #>   <chr>         <chr>  <chr>     <chr>   #> 1 in x only     extra  character NA      #> 2 type conflict value  integer   numeric

title: Compare two data frames — compare_data
text: Compare two data frames

title: Compare two data frames — compare_data
code: compare_data(   x,   y,   by = NULL,   context_rows = c(3L, 3L),   context_cols = everything(),   max_differences = Inf,   tolerance = .Machine$double.eps^0.5 )

title: Compare two data frames — compare_data
text: x, y Data frames compare. Optional character vector key columns match rows , like join. Key columns must exist data frames uniquely identify rows . NULL (default), rows matched position (row number). , output ordered key columns key columns always included output. context_rows Integer vector length two indicating number context rows include difference row. context_cols <tidy-select> Columns include context. max_differences Maximum number differing rows report. exceeded, first max_differences differing rows returned (message). tolerance Numeric tolerance comparing numeric values.

title: Compare two data frames — compare_data
text: data frame containing differences x y following columns: .row - row number original data frames .join_type - Whether row "x", "y", "" .diff_type - Whether row "diff" "context" row .source - diff rows, whether "x" "y" version; NA context rows Plus original data columns (context columns columns differences).

title: Compare two data frames — compare_data
text: Rows matched position (row number), key columns given. x y must share least one column, shared columns must compatible types; otherwise error thrown. Rows present one data frame always reported differences.

title: Compare two data frames — compare_data
code: x <- data.frame(id = 1:4, score = c(10, 20, 30, 40)) y <- data.frame(id = 1:4, score = c(10, 25, 30, 45))  # Rows are matched by position by default compare_data(x, y, context_rows = c(1L, 1L)) #> datadiff: 2 changed, 0 added, 0 removed rows across 1 column #> Tolerance: 1.49011611938477e-08 #>  #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>    .row .join_type .diff_type .source    id score #>   <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>   <int> <dbl> #> 1     1 both       context    NA          1    10 #> 2     2 both       diff       x           2    20 #> 3     2 both       diff       y           2    25 #> 4     3 both       context    NA          3    30 #> 5     4 both       diff       x           4    40 #> 6     4 both       diff       y           4    45  # Match on a key column instead of position compare_data(x, y, by = "id") #> datadiff: 2 changed, 0 added, 0 removed rows across 1 column #> Tolerance: 1.49011611938477e-08 #>  #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>    .row .join_type .diff_type .source    id score #>   <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>   <int> <dbl> #> 1     1 both       context    NA          1    10 #> 2     2 both       diff       x           2    20 #> 3     2 both       diff       y           2    25 #> 4     3 both       context    NA          3    30 #> 5     4 both       diff       x           4    40 #> 6     4 both       diff       y           4    45  # A numeric tolerance treats near-equal values as equal compare_data(x, y, tolerance = 10) #> datadiff: 0 changed, 0 added, 0 removed rows across 0 columns #> Tolerance: 10 #>  #> # A tibble: 0 × 6 #> # ℹ 6 variables: .row <int>, .join_type <chr>, .diff_type <chr>, .source <chr>, #> #   id <int>, score <dbl>

what: Compare groups between two data frames — compare_groups
title: Compare groups between two data frames — compare_groups
text: Compare groups two data frames

title: Compare groups between two data frames — compare_groups
code: compare_groups(x, y, group_cols)

title: Compare groups between two data frames — compare_groups
text: x, y Data frames compare group_cols <tidy-select> Columns use grouping

title: Compare groups between two data frames — compare_groups
text: data frame containing grouping columns two additional columns, in_x in_y, TRUE group values corresponding data frame FALSE otherwise. Records in_x in_y TRUE excluded output.

title: Compare groups between two data frames — compare_groups
code: x <- data.frame(team = c("a", "a", "b"), player = 1:3) y <- data.frame(team = c("a", "b", "c"), player = 4:6)  # Team values that appear in only one of the frames compare_groups(x, y, group_cols = team) #>   team  in_x in_y #> 1    c FALSE TRUE

title: Diff Data Frames — diffdata
text: High-level function compares two data frames renders result HTML report RStudio viewer browser.

title: Diff Data Frames — diffdata
code: diffdata(   x,   y,   by = NULL,   context_rows = c(3L, 3L),   context_cols = everything(),   max_differences = 10,   tolerance = .Machine$double.eps^0.5,   output_file = NULL )

title: Diff Data Frames — diffdata
text: x, y Data frames diff. Optional character vector key columns match rows , like join. NULL (default), rows matched position. See compare_data() details. context_rows Integer vector length two indicating number context rows include difference row. context_cols <tidy-select> Columns include context. max_differences Maximum number differing rows report. Defaults 10 (unlike compare_data(), reports everything) keep reports fast render. tolerance Numeric tolerance comparing numeric values. output_file Optional file path save HTML report. provided, report saved location instead opening viewer.

title: Diff Data Frames — diffdata
text: x y column differences (different names types), returns visible data frame describing differences (compare_columns()). Otherwise, invisibly returns diff data frame (compare_data()).

title: Diff Data Frames — diffdata
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() x <- data.frame(id = 1:5, score = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50)) y <- data.frame(id = 1:5, score = c(10, 25, 30, 40, 55))  # Opens a styled HTML diff report in the RStudio viewer or browser diffdata(x, y)  # Or write the report to a file instead of opening it diffdata(x, y, output_file = tempfile(fileext = ".html")) }

what: Extract the mismatching rows of a comparison — generateMismatchData
title: Extract the mismatching rows of a comparison — generateMismatchData
text: Given comparison rCompare() two original data frames, returns rows frame mismatch, dataCompareR's generateMismatchData(). Keyed comparisons return rows whose keys appear mismatch table, cleaned upper-case column names; keyless comparisons return rows mismatching positions original column names.

title: Extract the mismatching rows of a comparison — generateMismatchData
code: generateMismatchData(x, dfA, dfB, ...)

title: Extract the mismatching rows of a comparison — generateMismatchData
text: x comparison object returned rCompare(). dfA, dfB data frames compared. matched comparison names originally passed . ... Ignored, compatibility.

title: Extract the mismatching rows of a comparison — generateMismatchData
text: list two data frames named <name>_mm original inputs.

title: Extract the mismatching rows of a comparison — generateMismatchData
code: a <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1, 2, 3)) b <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1, 2.5, 3)) cmp <- rCompare(a, b, keys = "id") generateMismatchData(cmp, a, b) #> $a_mm #>   ID VALUE #> 2  2     2 #>  #> $b_mm #>   ID VALUE #> 2  2   2.5 #>

title: Vectorized Equality Test — is_equal
text: Element-wise equality comparison handles NA Inf values correctly, supports numeric tolerance floating-point comparisons.

title: Vectorized Equality Test — is_equal
code: is_equal(x, y, tolerance = .Machine$double.eps^0.5)

title: Vectorized Equality Test — is_equal
text: x, y Vectors compare. Must length, either can length 1. tolerance Numeric tolerance comparison. applies numeric values.

title: Vectorized Equality Test — is_equal
text: logical vector length x y, element TRUE corresponding elements equal (within tolerance numeric values) FALSE otherwise.

title: Vectorized Equality Test — is_equal
text: Comparison semantics: NA values equal , NaN values equal , NA equal NaN. Factors compared character values, factors different level sets can compared. Dates datetimes compared numerically, tolerance applies underlying scale (days Date, seconds POSIXct). Lists compared element-wise identical(); tolerance apply. x y incompatible types (e.g. numeric vs character), every element FALSE.

title: Vectorized Equality Test — is_equal
code: is_equal(c(1, 2, NA), c(1, 5, NA)) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  # NA equals NA and NaN equals NaN, but NA does not equal NaN is_equal(NA, NaN) #> [1] FALSE  # Tolerance controls how close numeric values must be is_equal(1, 1.001) #> [1] FALSE is_equal(1, 1.001, tolerance = 0.01) #> [1] TRUE

title: Print a dataCompareR-compatible comparison — print.datadiff_compare
text: Prints one-line comparison status followed head/tail excerpts mismatching column's rows, dataCompareR console format.

title: Print a dataCompareR-compatible comparison — print.datadiff_compare
code: # S3 method for class 'datadiff_compare' print(x, nVars = 5, nObs = 5, verbose = FALSE, ...)

title: Print a dataCompareR-compatible comparison — print.datadiff_compare
text: x comparison object returned rCompare(). nVars Number mismatched variables show start end variable list. nObs Number observations show start end variable's mismatch table. verbose TRUE, print every mismatching row every variable. ... Ignored, method compatibility.

title: Print a dataCompareR-compatible comparison — print.datadiff_compare
text: x, invisibly.

what: Compare two data frames, dataCompareR style — rCompare
title: Compare two data frames, dataCompareR style — rCompare
text: drop-replacement rCompare() archived 'dataCompareR' package. Compares two data frames — row position, key columns keys supplied — returns summary object shape dataCompareRobject, existing code scripts written 'dataCompareR' keep working.

title: Compare two data frames, dataCompareR style — rCompare
code: rCompare(   dfA,   dfB,   keys = NA,   roundDigits = NA,   mismatches = NA,   trimChars = FALSE,   tolerance = 0 )

title: Compare two data frames, dataCompareR style — rCompare
text: dfA, dfB Data frames compare. keys Character vector key columns used match rows, NA (default) match rows position. Keys must identify rows uniquely frame. roundDigits NA, round double columns many digits comparing. mismatches NA, maximum total number mismatching values allowed; error thrown comparison finds . trimChars TRUE, trim leading trailing whitespace character columns comparing. tolerance Numeric tolerance comparing numeric values, compare_data(). Defaults 0 (exact comparison, dataCompareR behaves).

title: Compare two data frames, dataCompareR style — rCompare
text: object class c("datadiff_compare", "dataCompareRobject"): list elements meta, colMatching, rowMatching, cleaninginfo, mismatches, matches, mirroring dataCompareR's return value.

title: Compare two data frames, dataCompareR style — rCompare
text: Following dataCompareR contract: column names cleaned make.names() matched case-insensitively (shared columns reported upper case, sorted alphabetically); factors compared character coercion recorded cleaninginfo; NA matches NA NaN matches NaN, NA match NaN; columns whose classes differ two frames reported mismatching every row. Without keys, longer frame truncated length shorter rows compared position. tolerance datadiffr extension (dataCompareR offers roundDigits). default 0 keeps faithful exact comparison.

title: Compare two data frames, dataCompareR style — rCompare
code: a <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1, 2, 3)) b <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1, 2.5, 3)) rCompare(a, b, keys = "id") #> All columns were compared, all rows were compared #> There are 1 mismatched variables: #> First and last 5 observations for the 1 mismatched variables #>   ID valueA valueB variable  typeA  typeB diffAB #> 1  2      2    2.5    VALUE double double   -0.5

what: Render a diff in a flexdashboard — render_diff
title: Render a diff in a flexdashboard — render_diff
text: Opens diff HTML report RStudio viewer (available) browser. Optionally saves file.

title: Render a diff in a flexdashboard — render_diff
code: render_diff(diff, output_file = NULL)  # S3 method for class 'datadiff_compare' render_diff(diff, output_file = NULL)  # Default S3 method render_diff(diff, output_file = NULL)

title: Render a diff in a flexdashboard — render_diff
text: diff Data frame returned compare_data(), containing .row, .join_type, .diff_type, .source, data columns. output_file Optional file path save HTML report. provided, report saved location instead (addition ) opening viewer.

title: Render a diff in a flexdashboard — render_diff
text: Invisibly returns path HTML file (either output_file provided, temporary file path).

title: Render a diff in a flexdashboard — render_diff
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() x <- data.frame(id = 1:5, score = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50)) y <- data.frame(id = 1:5, score = c(10, 25, 30, 40, 55))  diff <- compare_data(x, y) render_diff(diff) }

title: Save a comparison report — saveReport
text: Writes summary.datadiff_compare() report comparison disk R Markdown (optionally) rendered HTML, dataCompareR's saveReport().

title: Save a comparison report — saveReport
code: saveReport(   compareObject,   reportName,   reportLocation = ".",   HTMLReport = TRUE,   showInViewer = TRUE,   stylesheet = NA,   printAll = FALSE,   ... )

title: Save a comparison report — saveReport
text: compareObject comparison object returned rCompare(). reportName File name report, without extension. reportLocation Existing directory write report . HTMLReport TRUE, render report HTML (requires pandoc); intermediate markdown kept alongside . showInViewer TRUE session interactive, open rendered HTML report RStudio viewer browser. stylesheet Optional path CSS file HTML report, NA default style. printAll TRUE, per-column detail tables include every mismatching row instead first five. ... Ignored, compatibility.

title: Save a comparison report — saveReport
text: NULL, invisibly.

title: Save a comparison report — saveReport
code: a <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1, 2, 3)) b <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1, 2.5, 3)) cmp <- rCompare(a, b, keys = "id") saveReport(   cmp,   reportName = "example",   reportLocation = tempdir(),   showInViewer = FALSE )

title: Summarize a dataCompareR-compatible comparison — summary.datadiff_compare
text: Builds summary object dataCompareR's summary.dataCompareRobject(): list run metadata, column row matching counts, per-column mismatch details, printable markdown report.

title: Summarize a dataCompareR-compatible comparison — summary.datadiff_compare
code: # S3 method for class 'datadiff_compare' summary(object, mismatchCount = 5, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.datadiff_compare' print(x, ...)

title: Summarize a dataCompareR-compatible comparison — summary.datadiff_compare
text: object comparison object returned rCompare(). mismatchCount Maximum number rows keep per-column detail table. ... Ignored, method compatibility. x summary object returned summary.datadiff_compare().

title: Summarize a dataCompareR-compatible comparison — summary.datadiff_compare
text: list class c("summary.datadiff_compare", "summary.dataCompareRobject") dataCompareR summary fields.

title: Summarize a dataCompareR-compatible comparison — summary.datadiff_compare
text: Unlike dataCompareR (samples), detail tables deterministic: rows sorted decreasing absolute difference first mismatchCount kept.

title: Summarize a dataCompareR-compatible comparison — summary.datadiff_compare
code: a <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1, 2, 3)) b <- data.frame(id = 1:3, value = c(1, 2.5, 3)) summary(rCompare(a, b, keys = "id")) #>  #> Data Comparison #> =============== #>  #> Date comparison run: 2026-07-04 20:41:44   #> Comparison run on R version 4.6.1 (2026-06-24)   #> With datadiffr version 0.1.0   #>  #> Meta Summary #> ============ #>  #> |Dataset Name |Number of Rows |Number of Columns | #> |:------------|:--------------|:-----------------| #> |a            |3              |2                 | #> |b            |3              |2                 | #>  #> Variable Summary #> ================ #>  #> Number of columns in common: 2   #> Number of columns only in a: 0   #> Number of columns only in b: 0   #> Number of columns with a type mismatch: 0   #> Match keys : 1   - ID #>  #>  #> Row Summary #> =========== #>  #> Total number of rows read from a: 3   #> Total number of rows read from b: 3   #> Number of rows in common: 3   #> Number of rows dropped from a: 0   #> Number of rows dropped from b: 0   #>  #>  #> Data Values Comparison Summary #> ============================== #>  #> Number of columns compared with ALL rows equal: 0   #> Number of columns compared with SOME rows unequal: 1   #> Number of columns with missing value differences: 0   #>  #> Summary of columns with some rows unequal:  #>  #> |Column |Type (in a) |Type (in b) | # differences|Max difference | # NAs| #> |:------|:-----------|:-----------|-------------:|:--------------|-----:| #> |VALUE  |double      |double      |             1|0.5            |     0| #>  #>  #> Unequal column details #> ====================== #>  #> #### Column -  VALUE #>  #> | ID| VALUE (a)| VALUE (b)|Type (a) |Type (b) | Difference| #> |--:|---------:|---------:|:--------|:--------|----------:| #> |  2|         2|       2.5|double   |double   |       -0.5| #>

title: datadiffr 0.1.0
text: First release. datadiffr compares two data frames reports differences cell cell, configurable context rows around change, styled self-contained HTML report.

title: datadiffr 0.1.0
text: compare_data() compares two data frames returns differences tidy data frame, matching rows position key columns (=), configurable context rows around change. diffdata() runs comparison renders HTML report RStudio viewer, browser, file. compare_columns() reports column name type differences, compare_groups() compares group membership two frames. is_equal() provides tolerance-aware, vectorized equality handles NA, NaN, infinite values, compares factors, dates, datetimes, list-columns sensibly. Numeric comparisons accept tolerance, applied natural scale dates (days) datetimes (seconds).

title: datadiffr 0.1.0
text: Clean-room drop-replacements archived dataCompareR package — rCompare(), generateMismatchData(), saveReport(), print() / summary() methods — returning objects shape, existing scripts keep working. See vignette("migrating--datacomparer").
